test.command: MILESTONE="misc-infra-runtime-hardening-followups"; if [ -n "${MILESTONE:-}" ]; then ./.factory/library/verify-flow-report-finalization.sh "$MILESTONE" || exit 1; fi; scripts/test-openburnbar-swift.sh && scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh

typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Flow-report enforcement in `.factory/services.yaml` remains conditional on `MILESTONE` being set, so `commands.test` can still bypass enforcement if runtime invocation omits the environment variable.

Feature verification evidence does not prove validator-runtime finalization behavior: checks call `verify-flow-report-finalization.sh` directly instead of demonstrating negative/positive outcomes through the validator runtime finalization path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that infrastructure/runtime-hardening features must be assigned to a skill aligned with validator/runtime internals (or a dedicated infra skill), not a surface-specific switcher skill.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This feature was assigned `switcher-integration-worker` despite implementing validator runtime hardening, and review found contract-mismatched implementation/proof.

rejectedObservations.observation: Validator runtime ownership/integration points are under-documented in shared state.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; reviewer evidence identifies uncertainty but does not provide concrete, verified runtime locations suitable for immediate library or services updates.

override.justification: Remaining blockers require mission-runner/platform-level validator finalization instrumentation and mandatory milestone context injection, which are outside this repository. Repo-side enforcement has been integrated on controllable command paths.
